story.iWant: Git hooks that enforce quality gates before commits and pushes
story.soThat: code quality issues are caught locally before CI/CD.

tasks.task: Implement pre-push hook (Detekt + fast unit tests)
Verify hooks can be bypassed with --no-verify
Commit: "Add Git hooks for local quality gate enforcement"

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: .git-hooks/pre-push script runs Detekt + fast unit tests (<30s)
Hooks can be bypassed with --no-verify flag (but discouraged)
Clear error messages when hooks fail with remediation instructions
Hooks tested with intentional violations (formatting error, failing test)

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: FR008: Quality Gates with Configurable Profiles
doc.snippet: FR008 mandates automated quality gates that run ktlint, Detekt, Konsist, Pitest, and Testcontainers with fast (<30s), standard (<3min), and thorough (<15min) profiles. The hooks must surface clear diagnostics so developers resolve failures before CI.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR025 requires the framework to give developers hot reload, local feature flags, and pre-commit validation with automated fixes. Git hooks are the enforcement point for these local quality gates.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: The scope lists Git hooks as a core deliverable with pre-commit (ktlint <5s) and pre-push (Detekt + tests <30s) targets. These thresholds set the performance envelope for Story 1.10.

doc.title: Decision Architecture
doc.snippet: The architecture baseline includes Git hooks for quality gates (ktlint, Detekt) alongside the Constitutional TDD stack. Hooks must mirror the CI check pipeline so local and remote enforcement stay consistent.

doc.title: Project Development Guidelines
doc.snippet: The guidelines flag it as critical to avoid commits with --no-verify because Git hooks are mandatory quality gates. Story 1.10 must implement safeguards and education to uphold this rule.

doc.title: Contribution Guide
doc.snippet: The contribution guide defines the local pre-commit bundle (format, test naming, commit message) and states the <30s target. Hooks delivered in Story 1.10 must install and maintain this experience.

artifact.reason: Installer copies hook templates from .git-hooks/, backs up any existing hooks, and keeps local messages aligned with the template source of truth.
Gradle convention registers installGitHooks/uninstallGitHooks tasks, now copying the same templates that the shell installer uses so both entry points stay in sync.
One-command setup invokes install-git-hooks.sh and surfaces warnings. Hook implementation must remain idempotent so init-dev.sh stays reliable.
Fast feedback pipeline runs ktlintCheck, detekt, test, integrationTest, shellcheck. Hooks need to mirror these checks to satisfy AC5.
Wires ktlintCheck, detekt, test, kover tasks into the Gradle check lifecycle. Hook scripts should call the same tasks to keep enforcement consistent.

gradle.note: Quality tools configured via gradle/libs.versions.toml power the tasks invoked by the Git hooks and CI workflow.

tool.name: Git
tool.description: Git 2.30+ required for hook execution and --no-verify override.

tool.name: ShellCheck
tool.description: CI job lints hook scripts to prevent regressions.

constraints.constraint: Pre-commit executions must complete in under 5 seconds and pre-push in under 30 seconds (tech-spec performance budget).
Hook scripts must offer --no-verify bypass for emergencies while prominently discouraging routine use.
Installed hooks need to stay identical to the CI quality-gate stack (ktlint, detekt, tests, shellcheck).
Installer must detect and back up existing custom hooks before overwriting.
Hook failures must emit clear remediation guidance so developers can fix violations immediately.
Hook installation must be idempotent when invoked via init-dev.sh or gradlew installGitHooks.

interface.name: Git Hook Installer
interface.description: Interactive shell script that generates pre-commit and pre-push hooks, applies chmod +x, and prints remediation steps.

interface.name: Gradle Hook Task
interface.description: Generates hook scripts from the convention plugin. Story 1.10 must keep this task synchronized with the shell installer.

interface.name: Git Pre-Commit Hook
interface.description: Runs ktlint via ./gradlew ktlintCheck on staged Kotlin files and blocks commits on violations with remediation hints.

interface.name: Git Pre-Push Hook
interface.description: Executes detekt and ./gradlew test before pushes, failing fast when static analysis or tests break.

tests.standards:
Constitutional TDD still applies: every hook change must be backed by failing-first tests or scripted reproductions. Validate hook performance budgets (<5s / <30s), ensure violation scenarios provide actionable guidance, and confirm CI workflows stay aligned so local hooks match server-side enforcement.
Key references: docs/architecture.md (Constitutional TDD), CONTRIBUTING.md (Quality Gates), CLAUDE.md (no --no-verify policy).

ideas.test: **Formatting Violation Blocks Commit** – Stage a Kotlin file with ktlint issues, run git commit, verify pre-commit fails within 5 seconds and prints ktlint remediation.
**Static Analysis Failure Prevents Push** – Introduce a detekt violation, run git push, confirm pre-push aborts <30s and links to detekt report.
**Failing Tests Abort Push** – Introduce a failing unit test, run git push, ensure hook stops push and highlights ./gradlew test output.
**Bypass Workflow** – Run git commit --no-verify to confirm bypass works but prints warning discouraging routine use.
**CI Parity Check** – Execute .github/workflows/validate-hooks.yml locally (act) or in PR to confirm hook definitions match CI expectations.
**Error Messaging Regression Suite** – Trigger ktlint, detekt, and test failures, capture hook output, and assert remediation instructions are present for each scenario.
